title: Reset the Tipper After a Fault From the Visu_MCP_Dual Screen
summary: Use the operator station HMI Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen to review displayed alarm or informational messages, press RESET to reset the system after a fault, and press CYCLE START to restart the tipper operation cycle.
when_to_use: Use when a tipper fault has occurred and the operator is working from the primary operator station HMI screen (Visu_MCP_Dual) to reset the system and restart the tipper operation cycle.
not_for: Do not use for emergency stop reset; the source separately identifies ESTOP RESET as the control used after an E-stop.

Do not assume AUTO REF is always required as part of this procedure; the source states AUTO REF is enabled after the cycle has been reset, but this candidate does not establish it as a mandatory step for this fault reset procedure.
Do not use this runbook for maintenance-only manual control tasks on the Visu_ManControl screen.

responsibilities: Access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen on the operator station HMI.
Review the informational banner messages shown on the screen.
Press CYCLE START to restart the tipper operation cycle.

safety_notes: Use the primary operator station HMI screen intended for station operators.
The source distinguishes fault reset from E-stop reset; use the appropriate control for the condition present.
If the fault is not cleared after RESET, the supplied source section does not provide additional recovery steps.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the operator station HMI

access_required: Operator access to the operator station HMI
system_preconditions: The operator is at the Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen.
A fault condition has occurred that requires system reset from the HMI.

external_references.title: Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen overview
Tipper reset and axis reference functions

visual_references.description: Primary operator HMI screen for the tipper machine control panel showing RESET, CYCLE START, informational banner, and active-button indicators.
Operator station display controls showing how to access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

steps.title: Open the Visu_MCP_Dual screen and identify reset controls
steps.purpose: Get to the primary operator screen used for tipper movement control and fault reset, and visually confirm the RESET and CYCLE START controls before acting.
steps.instruction: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel (Visu_MCP_Dual) screen on the operator station HMI and identify the RESET and CYCLE START buttons on the screen.
steps.expected_result: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is displayed and the operator can locate the RESET and CYCLE START controls.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct primary operator screen is open and the RESET and CYCLE START buttons are visible. Green bars above buttons indicate which controls are active.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen or cannot identify the RESET and CYCLE START controls.

actions.label: Open Visu_MCP_Dual
actions.exact_action: Open the Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual") screen.

actions.label: Identify RESET and CYCLE START
actions.exact_action: Identify the RESET and CYCLE START buttons on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate the RESET and CYCLE START controls on the operator station control panel screen.
Use the display controls mapping to access the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed from the operator station HMI.
Escalate if the required controls are not visible or cannot be identified on the screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Tipper Machine Control Panel ("Visu_MCP_Dual" screen) has buttons that control tipper movement and allows for fault reset. The bars above the buttons illuminate green to indicate which buttons are active. NOTE: This is the primary screen that station operators will need to use.
F3 to "Visu_MCP_Dual" as the primary screen operators need to use.

steps.title: Review the informational banner messages
steps.purpose: Check the screen for displayed alarms or informational messages before resetting the system.
steps.instruction: Check the informational banner for alarms or informational messages. Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to review available messages.
steps.expected_result: The operator has reviewed the messages currently displayed in the informational banner.
steps.healthy_condition: The informational banner is visible and the operator can navigate through available messages using the arrows.
steps.failure_condition: The banner cannot be reviewed, messages cannot be navigated, or the operator cannot determine what messages are present.

actions.label: Inspect informational banner
actions.exact_action: Check the informational banner for alarms or informational messages.

actions.label: Navigate banner messages
actions.exact_action: Use the arrows on the right side of the banner to review available messages.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the informational banner and the arrows on the right side of the banner used to navigate messages.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the informational banner cannot be reviewed or message navigation is not functioning.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages, and the arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner.

steps.title: Press RESET after the fault
steps.purpose: Reset the system after a fault using the documented HMI control.
steps.instruction: Press RESET to reset the system after a fault.
steps.expected_result: The system is reset after the fault.
steps.healthy_condition: The fault condition is cleared by the RESET action and the system is ready for the next recovery action.
steps.failure_condition: The fault is not cleared after pressing RESET.

actions.label: Press RESET
actions.exact_action: Press RESET to reset the system after a fault.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate and press the RESET control on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the fault is not cleared after RESET.
Do not continue with this runbook if the condition is an emergency stop requiring ESTOP RESET instead of RESET.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The RESET button resets the system after a fault.

The RESET button resets the system after a fault. The AUTO REF button automatically references the axes of both tippers and is enabled after the cycle has been reset. The ESTOP RESET resets the system after an E-stop.

steps.title: Press CYCLE START to restart the tipper cycle
steps.purpose: Restart the tipper operation cycle after the fault reset is complete.
steps.instruction: Press CYCLE START to restart the tipper operation cycle.
steps.expected_result: The tipper operation cycle restarts from the HMI.
steps.healthy_condition: The system accepts CYCLE START and the tipper operation cycle resumes.
steps.failure_condition: The cycle does not restart after pressing CYCLE START.

actions.label: Press CYCLE START
actions.exact_action: Press CYCLE START to restart the tipper operation cycle.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Locate and press the CYCLE START control on the Visu_MCP_Dual screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the tipper operation cycle does not restart after CYCLE START.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press CYCLE START to restart the tipper operation cycle.

success_criteria: The system is reset after the fault.
The tipper operation cycle can be restarted from the HMI using CYCLE START.
healthy_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen is accessible and shows the required controls.
The informational banner can be reviewed using the banner arrows.
CYCLE START restarts the tipper operation cycle.
failure_conditions: The Visu_MCP_Dual screen cannot be accessed.
The informational banner cannot be reviewed.
The fault is not cleared after RESET.
The tipper operation cycle does not restart after CYCLE START.
escalation_guidance: If the fault is not cleared after RESET, escalate because the supplied source section does not provide additional recovery steps.
If the cycle does not restart after CYCLE START, escalate for further troubleshooting.
If the condition is an emergency stop rather than a normal fault, use the documented ESTOP RESET path instead of this runbook.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary visual reference for the Visu_MCP_Dual screen, including RESET, CYCLE START, informational banner, and active-button indicators.
Display controls and screen navigation used to access Visu_MCP_Dual.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The RESET button resets the system after a fault. Press CYCLE START to restart the tipper operation cycle.
An informational banner displays alarms or informational messages, and the arrows on the right side of the banner can be used to navigate through messages in the banner.

missing_details: The source packet does not provide additional troubleshooting steps if RESET does not clear the fault.
The source packet does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this procedure.
The source packet does not specify whether lockout/tagout is required for this procedure.
The source packet does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source packet does not define exact on-screen confirmation text indicating that the fault has cleared.
